raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) Pilot Project has benefited tens of thousands of Californians by making healthy foods more affordable for low-income families.",
    "The EBT Pilot Project aims to improve food security, nutrition, and health of CalFresh recipients.",
    "It provides up to $60 in nutrition incentives when CalFresh households purchase fresh fruits and vegetables with their CalFresh benefits.",
    "CalFresh recipients get $1 back on their EBT card for every $1 spent on fresh fruits and vegetables at participating retailers.",
    "The EBT Pilot Project benefits more than 43,000 CalFresh households, comprising an estimated 75,000 people.",
    "The EBT Pilot Project provided $1.9 million in rebates for fruits and vegetables in February 2024.",
    "More than 20% of households in California experienced food insecurity as of February 2024.",
    "The CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT program is associated with reducing hunger, improving health, and supporting the California agricultural economy.",
    "The EBT Pilot Project has improved food security and the health and well-being of participants.",
    "In Monterey County, where over 40% of residents face food insecurity, the CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Pilot Project addresses urgent needs for equitable access to nutritious food.",
    "Higher CalFresh benefits are correlated with lower levels of hunger."
  ]
}
```
